action.reasoning: Observation: the current Qwen LoRA pipeline runs, but the tiny run got 0.0 val score with many missing/invalid outputs; history already identified the most likely cause as poor supervision format and no prompt masking. Analysis: the highest-upside low-risk move is to patch the SFT data collator so loss is only on assistant target tokens, train a point-only canonical target (<point>x y</point>) to simplify generation, and harden parser/inference to recover point predictions reliably. We should keep the run small enough to finish quickly, then score locally end-to-end before deciding on eval. Decision: rewrite model.py/train.py around point-only masked SFT, run a 1-epoch subset LoRA training, produce val predictions, and score them.
action.command: python train.py --train_subset 1024 --epochs 1 --batch_size 1 --grad_accum 16 --lr 1e-4 --out checkpoints/qwen_lora_point && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/qwen_lora_point --output val_pred_qwen_point.json && python score_val.py --pred val_pred_qwen_point.json --gt /data/unified_val.jsonl
